contents.value:
**Source:** [Device Tree Specification](https://devicetree-specification.readthedocs.io/)

## pinctrl-0

Property name: `pinctrl-0`

Value type: `<prop-encoded-array>` encoded as a list of phandles

List of phandles referencing pin configuration nodes. These correspond to the pin configuration in state 0. State 0 is typically the default active state. Additional states are specified with `pinctrl-1`, `pinctrl-2`, etc.

---

**From binding** (\`qcom,sm8550-cci\`):

I/O pad configuration when PWM control is disabled

---

**Source:** Linux kernel Documentation

```
- pinctrl-1: Should contain UART mode pin ctrl

Else, the pin controller on the platform only can support pinmux control and
the GPIO control still has to rely on the dedicated GPIO controller such as
a legacy MediaTek SoC, MT7621. Please use the below properties.

- boot-gpios:	GPIO same to the pin as UART RXD and used to keep LOW when
		the device is powered up to enter proper bootstrap mode when
- pinctrl-names: Should be "default"
- pinctrl-0: Should contain UART mode pin ctrl
```
